name: Health & Wellness
description: A holistic course exploring the multiple dimensions of wellness — physical, mental, emotional, social, and environmental — and strategies for creating a balanced, healthy lifestyle.

modules.title: Dimensions of Wellness
modules.objectives: Identify the seven dimensions of wellness (physical, emotional, intellectual, social, spiritual, environmental, occupational) and explain how they interconnect.
modules.videoNote: YouTube — The Dimensions of Wellness Explained
modules.video2Note: YouTube — Holistic Health and Wellness Concepts
modules.practiceNote: Complete a wellness wheel self-assessment to identify your strongest and weakest dimensions
modules.assignment: Complete a personal wellness wheel assessment, rate yourself in each dimension (1–10), and write a 1-page reflection identifying your top two areas for growth and one action step for each.

modules.title: Physical Wellness & Exercise
modules.objectives: Explain how regular physical activity contributes to overall wellness, understand physical activity guidelines for adolescents, and identify barriers to exercise.
modules.videoNote: YouTube — Physical Wellness and Exercise Benefits
modules.practiceNote: Track your daily physical activity for one week and compare to the recommended 60 minutes per day
modules.assignment: Create a 2-week physical activity log tracking type, duration, and intensity of activity. Reflect on how physical activity impacted your mood and energy each day.

modules.title: Nutritional Wellness
modules.objectives: Apply MyPlate guidelines to evaluate and improve daily food choices, understand how nutrition affects energy, mood, and long-term health.
modules.video2Note: YouTube — How Diet Affects Mental and Physical Health
modules.practiceNote: Use the MyPlate Daily Checklist to plan and evaluate your meals for one day
modules.assignment: Track your food intake for 3 days using MyPlate, then write a reflection on how your eating habits align with nutritional wellness and identify two specific improvements.

modules.title: Mental & Emotional Health
modules.objectives: Define mental and emotional health, identify common mental health conditions in adolescents, and describe strategies for maintaining positive mental health.
modules.videoNote: YouTube — Understanding Mental Health in Teens
modules.video2Note: YouTube — How to Improve Your Mental Health
modules.practiceNote: Complete the NIMH teen mental health self-check and identify any concerns to discuss with a counselor
modules.assignment: Research and write a 1-page guide on two mental health strategies for adolescents (e.g., journaling, mindfulness), explaining the evidence for each and how to practice them.

modules.title: Stress Management
modules.objectives: Identify common stressors for teenagers, explain the physiological stress response, and apply multiple evidence-based stress management techniques.
modules.videoNote: YouTube — Stress Management Techniques for Teens
modules.video2Note: YouTube — Fight or Flight Response Explained
modules.practiceNote: Identify your top 3 stressors and practice one stress management technique for 5 days
modules.assignment: Create a personal stress management toolkit identifying your top three stressors and three evidence-based coping strategies with a plan for implementing each one.

modules.title: Sleep & Recovery
modules.objectives: Explain the stages of sleep, understand the recommended sleep duration for teenagers, and identify habits that promote and disrupt healthy sleep.
modules.videoNote: YouTube — Why Teens Need More Sleep
modules.video2Note: YouTube — Sleep Stages and Their Importance
modules.practiceNote: Track your sleep schedule for one week and evaluate your sleep quality using a sleep diary
modules.assignment: Track your sleep for 5 nights recording bedtime, wake time, and sleep quality. Identify 3 habits harming your sleep and write a 2-week sleep improvement plan.

modules.title: Social Health & Relationships
modules.objectives: Define social health, identify characteristics of healthy vs. unhealthy relationships, and understand effective communication skills for building positive connections.
modules.practiceNote: Complete the relationship health quiz at loveisrespect.org
modules.assignment: Write a personal reflection on the qualities you value in friendships and family relationships, describing one communication skill you want to develop and how you will practice it.

modules.title: Substance Use & Prevention
modules.objectives: Identify the short- and long-term health effects of tobacco, alcohol, and other substances on the developing brain, and apply refusal skills and protective strategies.
modules.readingNote: CDC — Alcohol Use Facts and Prevention
modules.videoNote: YouTube — Effects of Alcohol and Drugs on Teen Brain
modules.practiceNote: Research the CDC's statistics on teen substance use and identify three prevention strategies
modules.assignment: Create a prevention resource for peers that explains the brain effects of one substance (alcohol, nicotine, or cannabis), risk factors for addiction, and three refusal skill strategies.

modules.title: Sexual Health & Responsibility
modules.objectives: Understand basic reproductive health, STI prevention, and the importance of informed, consensual decision-making regarding sexual health.
modules.videoNote: YouTube — Sexual Health Education for Teens
modules.practiceNote: Review CDC's teen sexual health statistics and identify two evidence-based prevention strategies
modules.assignment: Write a fact sheet on one sexually transmitted infection (STI), covering transmission, symptoms, prevention, and testing, using credible public health sources.

modules.title: Environmental Health
modules.objectives: Identify how environmental factors (air quality, water safety, pollution) affect human health, and evaluate personal and community actions to protect environmental health.
modules.videoNote: YouTube — Environmental Health and Human Wellbeing
modules.video2Note: YouTube — How Pollution Affects Your Health
modules.practiceNote: Research the air quality index (AQI) in your local area and assess current environmental health risks
modules.assignment: Evaluate an environmental health issue in your community (e.g., air quality, water safety, waste management) and propose three evidence-based solutions or personal actions.

modules.title: Chronic Disease Prevention
modules.objectives: Identify the leading chronic diseases (heart disease, diabetes, cancer, obesity), understand modifiable risk factors, and apply prevention strategies through lifestyle choices.
modules.videoNote: YouTube — Chronic Disease Prevention Through Lifestyle
modules.video2Note: YouTube — Risk Factors for Chronic Disease
modules.practiceNote: Complete a personal chronic disease risk factor assessment using CDC resources
modules.assignment: Choose one chronic disease, research its risk factors, write a 2-page prevention plan that applies diet, exercise, sleep, and stress management strategies for a teenager.

modules.title: Consumer Health & Healthcare
modules.objectives: Understand how to evaluate health information sources, navigate basic healthcare systems, and make informed decisions as a health consumer.
modules.readingNote: MedlinePlus — Health Literacy and Consumer Health
modules.videoNote: YouTube — How to Evaluate Health Information Online
modules.practiceNote: Evaluate two health websites for credibility using the CRAAP test criteria
modules.assignment: Evaluate three health information sources (website, social media post, news article) using criteria for credibility, accuracy, and bias, and write a summary of your findings.

modules.title: Community & Global Health
modules.objectives: Identify social determinants of health, understand how community and global factors affect population health, and recognize the role of public health institutions.
modules.videoNote: YouTube — Social Determinants of Health Explained
modules.practiceNote: Research a current global health issue on the WHO website and describe its impact on a specific population
modules.assignment: Research one social determinant of health (e.g., income, education, housing) and write a 1-page essay explaining how it affects health outcomes and what communities can do to address it.

modules.title: Creating a Personal Wellness Plan
modules.objectives: Synthesize all wellness dimensions into a comprehensive, personalized wellness plan with specific goals, strategies, and tracking methods for maintaining lifelong health.
modules.videoNote: YouTube — Creating a Personal Wellness Plan
modules.practiceNote: Use the SAMHSA wellness plan template to draft your personal wellness goals for each dimension
modules.assignment: Create a comprehensive personal wellness plan addressing all wellness dimensions, with two SMART goals per dimension, specific action steps, a timeline, and a method for tracking progress.

quizQuestions.question: How many dimensions of wellness does SAMHSA identify?
quizQuestions.explanation: SAMHSA identifies 8 dimensions of wellness: emotional, financial, social, spiritual, occupational, physical, intellectual, and environmental.

quizQuestions.question: Which best describes the concept of 'holistic wellness'?
options: Recognizing that all wellness dimensions are interconnected and equally important
Prioritizing spiritual health above all others
Achieving perfect health in every dimension
quizQuestions.explanation: Holistic wellness acknowledges that all health dimensions are interconnected — weakness in one can affect all others.

quizQuestions.question: Intellectual wellness involves:
options: Engaging in creative and stimulating mental activities and lifelong learning
quizQuestions.explanation: Intellectual wellness is nurtured through curiosity, learning, problem-solving, and engaging in mentally stimulating activities.

quizQuestions.question: A wellness wheel is used to:
options: Visually assess balance across all wellness dimensions
quizQuestions.explanation: A wellness wheel allows individuals to visually assess their perceived wellbeing in each dimension and identify areas for growth.

quizQuestions.question: Occupational wellness refers to:
options: Finding meaning and satisfaction in your work or studies
quizQuestions.explanation: Occupational wellness involves deriving meaning, satisfaction, and purpose from work, school, or other productive activities.

quizQuestions.question: How much daily physical activity do adolescents need according to health guidelines?
quizQuestions.explanation: The CDC recommends that adolescents get at least 60 minutes of moderate-to-vigorous physical activity every day.

quizQuestions.question: Which type of physical activity is recommended at least 3 days per week for teens?
quizQuestions.explanation: Muscle and bone-strengthening activities (resistance training, gymnastics) are recommended at least 3 days per week for teens.

quizQuestions.question: A common barrier to exercise for teenagers is:
options: Lack of time due to academic and social commitments
quizQuestions.explanation: Time constraints from school, homework, and social activities are among the most frequently cited barriers to teen physical activity.

quizQuestions.question: Regular physical activity improves which aspect of mental wellness?
options: It has no effect on mental health
It improves mood, reduces anxiety and depression, and enhances self-esteem
quizQuestions.explanation: Regular physical activity triggers endorphin release, reduces cortisol, and improves mood, self-esteem, and mental health outcomes.

quizQuestions.question: Sedentary behavior refers to:
options: Time spent in activities with very low energy expenditure (sitting, screen time)
Any exercise lasting less than 30 minutes
quizQuestions.explanation: Sedentary behavior includes prolonged sitting, screen use, and other very low-energy activities that can harm health even in exercising individuals.

quizQuestions.question: MyPlate recommends that half your plate be filled with:
quizQuestions.explanation: The USDA MyPlate guideline recommends filling half of every meal's plate with fruits and vegetables.

quizQuestions.question: How does poor nutrition most directly affect mental wellness?
options: It has no connection to mental health
Nutrient deficiencies can impair brain function, mood, and cognitive performance
quizQuestions.explanation: Nutritional deficiencies (e.g., iron, B vitamins, omega-3s) can negatively impact brain chemistry, mood regulation, and cognitive function.

quizQuestions.question: Which eating habit best supports nutritional wellness?
options: Eating a variety of whole, minimally processed foods from all food groups
quizQuestions.explanation: Nutritional wellness is supported by variety, balance, and adequacy — choosing whole foods from all food groups in appropriate amounts.

quizQuestions.question: Mindful eating involves:
options: Paying full attention to hunger cues, flavors, and eating without distraction
quizQuestions.explanation: Mindful eating means eating with full attention, savoring food, and listening to hunger and satiety cues without distraction.

quizQuestions.question: Hydration's impact on wellness includes:
options: Supporting cognitive function, mood, digestion, and physical performance
quizQuestions.explanation: Adequate hydration supports brain function, mood regulation, digestive health, temperature regulation, and physical performance.

quizQuestions.question: Mental health is best defined as:
options: The absence of all mental illness
Emotional, psychological, and social well-being that affects thinking, feeling, and behavior
quizQuestions.explanation: Mental health encompasses emotional, psychological, and social well-being, affecting how we think, feel, and interact with the world.

quizQuestions.question: Anxiety disorders are the most common mental health condition in:
quizQuestions.explanation: Anxiety disorders are the most prevalent mental health conditions in adolescents and young adults, often beginning in childhood or teen years.

quizQuestions.question: Which is NOT a healthy coping strategy for mental health challenges?
options: Using alcohol to reduce feelings of anxiety
quizQuestions.explanation: Alcohol is an unhealthy and potentially harmful coping mechanism that masks symptoms rather than addressing mental health challenges.

quizQuestions.question: Stigma around mental health prevents many teens from seeking help because:
options: Mental health treatment is illegal for teens
They fear judgment, social exclusion, or being labeled as weak
Therapy is too expensive for all teens
Mental illness is not taken seriously by doctors
quizQuestions.explanation: Social stigma — fear of being judged, labeled, or excluded — is one of the primary barriers preventing teens from seeking mental health support.

quizQuestions.question: A protective factor for teen mental health includes:
options: Social support from caring adults and peers
quizQuestions.explanation: Strong social support from family, peers, and trusted adults is one of the most significant protective factors for adolescent mental health.

quizQuestions.question: The body's 'fight or flight' response is triggered by which hormone?
quizQuestions.explanation: The fight-or-flight response involves the release of cortisol and adrenaline from the adrenal glands in response to perceived threats.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is an evidence-based stress management technique?
options: Deep breathing exercises and mindfulness meditation
quizQuestions.explanation: Deep breathing and mindfulness meditation activate the parasympathetic nervous system, reducing physiological stress responses.

quizQuestions.question: Chronic stress can harm physical health by:
options: Elevating cortisol levels, suppressing immune function, and increasing disease risk
quizQuestions.explanation: Prolonged cortisol elevation from chronic stress suppresses the immune system, raises blood pressure, and increases risk of chronic disease.

quizQuestions.question: Which type of stress can sometimes improve performance?
quizQuestions.explanation: Eustress is positive, motivating stress that enhances focus and performance — like the excitement before a big presentation or race.

quizQuestions.question: Which time management strategy most directly reduces academic stress?
options: Breaking large assignments into smaller, scheduled tasks
quizQuestions.explanation: Breaking tasks into manageable pieces and scheduling them reduces the overwhelming nature of large assignments, decreasing stress.

quizQuestions.question: How many hours of sleep per night do teenagers need?
quizQuestions.explanation: The CDC and American Academy of Sleep Medicine recommend 8–10 hours of sleep per night for teenagers aged 13–18.

quizQuestions.question: REM (Rapid Eye Movement) sleep is especially important for:
quizQuestions.explanation: REM sleep plays a critical role in consolidating memories, processing emotions, and supporting learning and cognitive function.

quizQuestions.question: Blue light from screens before bed disrupts sleep by:
options: Suppressing melatonin production and delaying sleep onset
quizQuestions.explanation: Blue light mimics daylight, suppressing melatonin (the sleep hormone), making it harder to fall asleep at a natural bedtime.

quizQuestions.question: Which sleep hygiene habit promotes better sleep quality?
quizQuestions.explanation: Going to bed and waking at consistent times strengthens the circadian rhythm, improving sleep quality and ease of falling asleep.

quizQuestions.question: Sleep deprivation in teens is associated with:
options: Impaired concentration, mood instability, and increased health risks
quizQuestions.explanation: Insufficient sleep impairs cognitive function, emotional regulation, immune response, and is linked to increased risk of anxiety and depression.

quizQuestions.question: A healthy relationship is characterized by:
options: Mutual respect, trust, honesty, and support
quizQuestions.explanation: Healthy relationships are built on mutual respect, trust, open communication, honesty, and support for each other's wellbeing.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is a warning sign of an unhealthy relationship?
options: One partner attempting to control the other's behavior
quizQuestions.explanation: Controlling behavior, including monitoring movements, isolating from friends, or making decisions for the partner, is a warning sign of abuse.

quizQuestions.question: 'I' statements in communication are effective because they:
options: Blame the other person for problems
Express feelings without accusation, reducing defensiveness
quizQuestions.explanation: 'I feel... when...' statements communicate feelings without blaming others, reducing defensiveness and improving resolution.

quizQuestions.question: Social wellness is most strongly associated with:
options: Having the most social media followers
Meaningful connections and a supportive social network
Being the most popular person at school
quizQuestions.explanation: Social wellness is about the quality of relationships and social support networks, not quantity of connections or popularity.

quizQuestions.question: Active listening involves:
options: Thinking about your response while the other person talks
Fully concentrating on, understanding, and responding to the speaker
Talking over others to make your point
quizQuestions.explanation: Active listening requires full attention to the speaker, withholding judgment, and reflecting back what was heard before responding.

quizQuestions.question: Nicotine in tobacco products affects the developing teen brain by:
options: Having no effect on teenage brains
Disrupting formation of brain circuits that control attention, learning, and impulse control
quizQuestions.explanation: Nicotine is highly addictive and disrupts developing brain circuits responsible for attention, mood regulation, learning, and impulse control in teens.

quizQuestions.question: The minimum legal drinking age in the United States is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The legal drinking age in all 50 US states is 21 years old.

quizQuestions.question: Which factor most protects teens from substance use?
options: Strong family bonds and healthy peer relationships
quizQuestions.explanation: Strong family connections, positive peer relationships, and school engagement are among the strongest protective factors against teen substance use.

quizQuestions.question: A refusal skill for peer pressure might include:
options: Accepting substances to avoid social rejection
Confidently saying 'No thanks' and suggesting an alternative activity
Pretending to agree and then using the substance later
quizQuestions.explanation: Effective refusal skills include a clear, firm 'No,' offering an alternative, and leaving the situation if pressure continues.

quizQuestions.question: Electronic cigarettes (e-cigarettes) are harmful to teens because:
options: They contain nicotine and aerosol chemicals harmful to the developing brain and lungs
They are approved as safe by the FDA for teens
quizQuestions.explanation: E-cigarettes contain nicotine, ultrafine particles, and other chemicals harmful to the developing teen brain and respiratory system.

quizQuestions.question: Consent in sexual health means:
options: Automatic agreement in a long-term relationship
Clear, ongoing, and voluntary agreement from all partners before any sexual activity
Agreement given under pressure is acceptable
quizQuestions.explanation: Consent must be clear, ongoing, freely given, and can be withdrawn at any time; it cannot be assumed from silence or pressure.

quizQuestions.question: Which method is most effective at preventing both STIs and unintended pregnancy?
quizQuestions.explanation: Consistent, correct condom use provides significant protection against both sexually transmitted infections and unintended pregnancy.

quizQuestions.question: HPV (Human Papillomavirus) is unique among common STIs because:
options: There is no test or vaccine
It can be prevented by a vaccine (Gardasil)
quizQuestions.explanation: HPV is the only STI for which a highly effective preventive vaccine (Gardasil) is available and recommended for preteens and teens.

quizQuestions.question: Why is sexual health education important for adolescents?
options: It provides accurate information to support informed decision-making and reduce STIs and teen pregnancy
It is only for sexually active teens
quizQuestions.explanation: Comprehensive sexual health education equips teens with accurate knowledge to make informed decisions, reducing rates of STIs and unintended pregnancy.

quizQuestions.question: Regular STI testing is recommended for sexually active teens because:
options: Many STIs have no symptoms and can be treated or managed when detected early
quizQuestions.explanation: Many STIs (chlamydia, HIV, herpes) can be asymptomatic; early detection through testing allows timely treatment and prevents spread.

quizQuestions.question: The Air Quality Index (AQI) measures:
options: Concentration of pollutants in outdoor air and associated health risk
quizQuestions.explanation: The AQI measures the concentrations of five major pollutants in outdoor air and translates them into a health risk scale.

quizQuestions.question: Lead exposure in children is most commonly linked to:
options: Old lead-based paint and contaminated water in older homes
quizQuestions.explanation: Lead exposure in children most commonly occurs from deteriorating lead-based paint in pre-1978 homes and lead-contaminated water from old pipes.

quizQuestions.question: Which individual action best supports environmental health?
options: Reducing, reusing, and recycling to minimize waste
quizQuestions.explanation: Reducing, reusing, and recycling limits waste production, conserves resources, and reduces environmental pollutants.

quizQuestions.question: Climate change is linked to human health by:
options: Increasing heat-related illness, air pollution, infectious disease spread, and natural disasters
quizQuestions.explanation: Climate change worsens air quality, expands ranges of disease vectors, increases extreme weather events, and exacerbates heat-related illness.

quizQuestions.question: Access to safe drinking water is important for public health because contaminated water can cause:
options: Waterborne diseases such as cholera, typhoid, and diarrheal illness
quizQuestions.explanation: Contaminated water transmits serious waterborne pathogens that cause diarrheal illness, cholera, typhoid, and other diseases globally.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is a modifiable risk factor for chronic disease?
quizQuestions.explanation: Physical inactivity is a modifiable risk factor — lifestyle changes can significantly reduce its contribution to chronic disease risk.

quizQuestions.question: Type 2 diabetes is most strongly associated with which lifestyle factor?
quizQuestions.explanation: Obesity and physical inactivity are the primary modifiable risk factors for type 2 diabetes, a preventable chronic disease.

quizQuestions.question: The leading cause of death in the United States is:
quizQuestions.explanation: Heart disease has been the leading cause of death in the United States for most of the past century, responsible for approximately 1 in 4 deaths.

quizQuestions.question: Regular aerobic exercise reduces the risk of chronic disease by:
options: Improving insulin sensitivity, reducing inflammation, and strengthening the cardiovascular system
Having no effect on disease risk
quizQuestions.explanation: Regular aerobic exercise improves insulin sensitivity, reduces inflammation, lowers blood pressure, and strengthens the heart, reducing chronic disease risk.

quizQuestions.question: Which diet pattern is most strongly associated with reduced chronic disease risk?
options: Plant-rich diet with fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and lean proteins
quizQuestions.explanation: Diets rich in fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and lean proteins and low in processed foods are associated with the lowest chronic disease risk.

quizQuestions.question: A reliable health information website can be identified by:
options: Government (.gov), educational (.edu), or reputable nonprofit (.org) domains with cited authors
The most popular social media post
quizQuestions.explanation: Credible health sources include government, academic, and established medical organization websites with identified authors and cited evidence.

quizQuestions.question: Health literacy is defined as:
options: The ability to obtain, process, and understand health information to make informed decisions
Following all health advice on social media
quizQuestions.explanation: Health literacy is the capacity to find, understand, and use health information effectively to make decisions about care and lifestyle.

quizQuestions.question: Preventive healthcare includes which of the following?
options: Waiting until illness is severe before seeing a doctor
Annual checkups, vaccinations, and screenings before disease develops
Avoiding healthcare providers unless in emergency
quizQuestions.explanation: Preventive healthcare includes routine checkups, immunizations, and health screenings that detect or prevent disease before it progresses.

quizQuestions.question: Which is a red flag for health misinformation?
options: Promises of miraculous cures or treatments not supported by evidence
quizQuestions.explanation: Miraculous cure claims, lack of cited evidence, anonymous sources, and sensationalized language are hallmarks of health misinformation.

quizQuestions.question: Generic drugs are:
options: Chemically identical to brand-name drugs but typically less expensive
quizQuestions.explanation: Generic drugs contain the same active ingredients in the same doses as brand-name drugs and are FDA-approved equivalents at lower cost.

quizQuestions.question: Social determinants of health include:
options: Income, education, neighborhood, employment, and access to healthcare
quizQuestions.explanation: Social determinants are the conditions in which people are born, grow, work, and live that significantly affect health outcomes.

quizQuestions.question: The World Health Organization (WHO) defines health as:
options: The absence of disease or infirmity only
A state of complete physical, mental, and social well-being, not merely absence of disease
quizQuestions.explanation: WHO defines health as complete physical, mental, and social well-being — a positive concept that goes beyond the absence of illness.

quizQuestions.question: Health equity means:
options: Everyone gets exactly the same healthcare
Every person has a fair opportunity to attain their highest level of health
Only wealthy people receive quality care
Health is equally distributed by chance
quizQuestions.explanation: Health equity means addressing disparities so all people have fair opportunities to achieve their full health potential regardless of social circumstances.

quizQuestions.question: Public health institutions like the CDC primarily work to:
options: Prevent disease and protect the health of populations through policy, research, and education
quizQuestions.explanation: The CDC and similar public health organizations focus on population-level disease prevention, health promotion, and emergency response.

quizQuestions.question: Which global health issue causes the most childhood deaths worldwide?
options: Preventable and treatable infectious diseases and malnutrition
quizQuestions.explanation: Preventable and treatable conditions including pneumonia, diarrhea, and malaria alongside malnutrition are leading causes of global childhood mortality.

quizQuestions.question: A personal wellness plan should include goals for:
options: All dimensions of wellness with specific action steps
quizQuestions.explanation: A comprehensive wellness plan addresses all dimensions of health with specific, measurable goals and action plans for each.

quizQuestions.question: SMART goals in a wellness plan should be:
options: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound
Set by others without your input
quizQuestions.explanation: SMART goals provide clear, structured targets with defined timelines that make progress trackable and achievable.

quizQuestions.question: The most important factor for long-term success with a wellness plan is:
options: Consistency and gradual progress over time
quizQuestions.explanation: Sustainable wellness improvement requires consistent effort and gradual progress — not perfection — tailored to individual needs and preferences.

quizQuestions.question: Tracking progress in a wellness plan is important because it:
options: Provides feedback, maintains motivation, and allows plan adjustments
Only applies to physical fitness goals
quizQuestions.explanation: Tracking provides data on what is working, maintains accountability and motivation, and allows evidence-based plan adjustments.

quizQuestions.question: A personal wellness plan is most effective when it:
options: Is personalized to individual values, interests, and realistic lifestyle factors
Focuses only on areas of strength
quizQuestions.explanation: Personalized wellness plans that align with individual values, interests, and realistic constraints are most sustainable and effective.

questions.question: SAMHSA's model identifies how many dimensions of wellness?
questions.explanation: SAMHSA identifies 8 dimensions: emotional, financial, social, spiritual, occupational, physical, intellectual, and environmental.

questions.question: The CDC recommends how many minutes of daily physical activity for teenagers?
questions.explanation: The CDC recommends at least 60 minutes of moderate-to-vigorous physical activity daily for adolescents.

questions.question: Mental health is best described as:
options: The absence of any mental disorder
questions.explanation: Mental health is a positive concept encompassing emotional, psychological, and social well-being, not just the absence of illness.

questions.question: The fight-or-flight stress response involves release of:
questions.explanation: The fight-or-flight response releases cortisol and adrenaline (epinephrine) from the adrenal glands in response to stress.

questions.question: How many hours of sleep per night do teenagers need?
questions.explanation: The American Academy of Sleep Medicine recommends 8–10 hours of sleep per night for teenagers aged 13–18.

questions.question: Blue light from screens disrupts sleep by:
options: Suppressing melatonin and delaying sleep onset
questions.explanation: Blue light from screens suppresses melatonin production, making it harder to fall asleep at the normal bedtime.

questions.question: A healthy relationship is characterized by:
options: Mutual respect, trust, and open communication
questions.explanation: Healthy relationships are built on mutual respect, trust, honest communication, and support for each other's wellbeing and autonomy.

questions.question: MyPlate recommends filling how much of your plate with fruits and vegetables?
questions.explanation: USDA's MyPlate recommends making half of every meal's plate fruits and vegetables to support health and disease prevention.

questions.question: Which substance is both addictive and harmful to the developing teen brain?
options: Caffeine in moderate amounts from natural sources
questions.explanation: Nicotine is highly addictive and disrupts the developing adolescent brain's circuits for attention, learning, and impulse control.

questions.question: Which is a warning sign of an unhealthy relationship?
options: Controlling and isolating behavior by one partner
questions.explanation: Controlling, isolating, or monitoring a partner's behavior are red flags for emotional or physical abuse in a relationship.

questions.question: Eustress differs from distress because eustress:
options: Is positive, motivating stress that improves performance
questions.explanation: Eustress is positive, energizing stress that motivates and enhances performance, while distress is negative and can harm health.

questions.question: Consent in sexual health requires that agreement be:
options: Clear, voluntary, and can be withdrawn at any time
Assumed if no refusal is expressed
questions.explanation: Consent must be clearly given, freely chosen without pressure, ongoing, and can be withdrawn at any moment by any party.

questions.question: Chronic stress can harm physical health by:
options: Elevating cortisol, suppressing immunity, and increasing heart disease risk
questions.explanation: Sustained cortisol elevation from chronic stress weakens the immune system, raises blood pressure, and increases chronic disease risk.

questions.question: REM sleep is especially important for:
questions.explanation: REM sleep is critical for processing emotions, consolidating memories, and supporting learning and cognitive function.

questions.question: Which factor most protects teens from substance use?
options: Strong family bonds and positive peer relationships
questions.explanation: Strong family connections, caring adults, and positive peer relationships are among the strongest protective factors against adolescent substance use.

questions.question: Social determinants of health include which factors?
options: Income, education, neighborhood, and access to healthcare
questions.explanation: Social determinants — conditions like income, education, housing, and environment — profoundly shape health outcomes across populations.

questions.question: The leading cause of death in the United States is:
questions.explanation: Heart disease has been the leading cause of death in the US for decades, accounting for approximately 1 in 4 deaths annually.

questions.question: A credible health information website is most likely to have which feature?
options: Cited authors, peer-reviewed sources, and a .gov or .edu domain
questions.explanation: Credible health sites provide clear author credentials, citations from peer-reviewed sources, and are often associated with government or educational institutions.

questions.question: HPV is unique among STIs because:
options: A preventive vaccine (Gardasil) exists and is recommended for teens
questions.explanation: HPV is the only STI for which a highly effective preventive vaccine is available and recommended for preteens and teenagers.

questions.question: A wellness plan is most effective when it:
options: Is personalized to individual values and realistic lifestyle factors
Changes completely each week without tracking
questions.explanation: Personalized, realistic wellness plans aligned with individual interests and values are the most sustainable and effective over the long term.

questions.question: The Air Quality Index (AQI) is a measure of:
options: Outdoor air pollutant concentrations and their health implications
questions.explanation: The AQI translates outdoor air pollutant concentrations into a standardized scale that communicates health risk to the public.

questions.question: Health equity means:
options: All people have a fair opportunity to achieve their highest possible health
Only the wealthy deserve health resources
Health outcomes are determined solely by genetics
questions.explanation: Health equity means removing barriers so everyone has a fair opportunity to achieve optimal health, regardless of social circumstances.

questions.question: Preventive healthcare includes:
options: Routine checkups, vaccinations, and health screenings
questions.explanation: Preventive care detects risk factors and conditions early through regular checkups, vaccinations, and recommended health screenings.

questions.question: Physical inactivity is classified as a chronic disease risk factor that is:
questions.explanation: Physical inactivity is a modifiable risk factor — increasing daily activity significantly reduces chronic disease risk regardless of age.

questions.question: WHO defines health as:
options: Absence of disease or infirmity only
A complete state of physical, mental, and social well-being
questions.explanation: The World Health Organization defines health as a state of complete physical, mental, and social well-being, not merely the absence of disease.

questions.question: Which sleep habit is most effective for improving sleep quality?
options: Varying sleep and wake times daily
questions.explanation: A consistent sleep-wake schedule strengthens the circadian rhythm and is the most evidence-based sleep hygiene practice.

questions.question: Active listening improves relationships because it:
options: Allows you to prepare your response while someone talks
Demonstrates full attention and understanding, reducing conflict
Requires you to talk more than listen
Is only used in professional settings
questions.explanation: Active listening — giving full attention and reflecting back what's heard — reduces misunderstandings and strengthens relationship trust.

questions.question: E-cigarettes are harmful to teens because they contain:
options: Nicotine and harmful aerosol chemicals that damage the developing brain and lungs
questions.explanation: E-cigarettes contain nicotine, ultrafine particles, and other chemicals that harm the developing teen brain and respiratory system.

questions.question: Type 2 diabetes is most linked to which modifiable lifestyle factors?
options: Too much sleep and vitamin excess
Excessive exercise and high protein intake
questions.explanation: Obesity and physical inactivity are the primary modifiable risk factors for type 2 diabetes, making it largely preventable.

questions.question: A SMART wellness goal must be:
options: Vague and flexible to allow any outcome
Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound
Set by a healthcare provider only
questions.explanation: SMART goals provide clear, actionable targets with specific criteria that make progress measurable and achievement realistic.

questions.question: Occupational wellness in a high school student most directly relates to:
options: Finding meaning and purpose in academic work and extracurricular activities
questions.explanation: For students, occupational wellness means finding satisfaction, purpose, and engagement in academic work and activities.

questions.question: Waterborne diseases are caused by:
options: Consuming contaminated water containing pathogens like bacteria or parasites
questions.explanation: Waterborne diseases result from pathogens (bacteria, viruses, parasites) in contaminated water that cause illnesses like cholera and typhoid.

questions.question: The most effective approach to stress management includes:
options: Using a variety of healthy coping strategies tailored to the stressor type
Relying on substances to reduce stress
questions.explanation: Using diverse, healthy coping strategies — exercise, social support, mindfulness, time management — matched to different stressors is most effective.

questions.question: Mindful eating involves:
options: Paying full attention to hunger, flavors, and eating without distraction
questions.explanation: Mindful eating means eating with full awareness of hunger and satiety cues, flavors, and the eating experience without distraction.

questions.question: Tracking progress in a personal wellness plan is important because it:
options: Provides feedback that maintains motivation and guides plan adjustments
Replaces the need for professional healthcare
questions.explanation: Progress tracking creates accountability, reveals what's working, maintains motivation, and guides evidence-based plan improvements.
